name: Bob Berg
abstract: Robert Berg (April 7, 1951 – December 5, 2002) was an American jazz saxophonist.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Art Blakey and The Jazz Messengers/ Horace Silver Quintet/ Freddie Hubbard
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: The Legacy of Miles

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: At the time, uh, in our group was uh, the late Bob Berg on tenor saxophone
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pierre Boussaguet
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...I just wrote down a short list of musicians you worked with during your career... Bob Berg... What an impressive list. You must have so many memories, working with all those giants of jazz. [INTERVIEWEE] Yes of course...
about_quotes.doc_title: Pierre Boussaguet interviewed by Matthieu Jaffre, Aix en Provence, France, July 28, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Adrian Cunningham
about_quotes.evidence: I really love — yeah, I think Bob Berg’s sound is incredible. And he’s someone I would have loved to have heard live.
about_quotes.doc_title: Adrian Cunningham interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 31, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: The main crew was Brecker – was Michael, Grossman, Bob Berg. We were the crew.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Dobbins
about_quotes.evidence: Cedar Walton’s Eastern Rebellion Band and he had Bob Berg working in the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Dobbins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rochester, New York, January 11, 2012
